Website SEO Foundations for a New Business

By Mahatheer Muhammadh 2 min read
Table of Contents
  1. Make Every Page Purposeful
  2. Use Metadata and Structured Data Carefully
  3. Keep the Site Crawlable

Make Every Page Purposeful

Each page should target a clear purpose: explain a service, introduce the business, show work, or help visitors contact you. Search visibility improves when page intent is obvious.

Key Takeaways
  • Every page needs a clear purpose.
  • Search engines evaluate page intent.
  • Avoid thin duplicate pages.

Use Metadata and Structured Data Carefully

Titles, descriptions, canonical tags, Open Graph data, and structured schema help machines understand the page. They should be accurate and match visible content.

Key Takeaways
  • Metadata should be unique per page.
  • Schema should match visible facts.
  • Canonical URLs prevent confusion.

Keep the Site Crawlable

Search engines need clear internal links, a sitemap, accessible pages, and images with alt text. Avoid hiding important content behind scripts that crawlers cannot access.

Key Takeaways
  • Internal links help discovery.
  • Sitemaps support crawling.
  • Visible content is safer for SEO.
Key Takeaways

Full Article Summary

  • New sites need clear page intent.
  • Unique metadata supports search understanding.
  • Structured data should be accurate.
  • Internal links and sitemap help discovery.
  • Accessible content is better for users and search engines.

Frequently Asked Questions

Start with clear page titles, meta descriptions, headings, internal links, sitemap, robots file, local business details, and useful service content.

A sitemap is recommended because it helps search engines discover important pages quickly.

Use one clear H1 per page, then organize sections with logical H2 and H3 headings.

Yes. Alt text improves accessibility and helps search engines understand image context.

Yes. Metadata, structure, content, sitemap, and schema can be prepared before launch.
